enger trains are accomplished in a
fashion.


Long delays are very unpleasant
in an out Of the way place like the
o. r. & N. junction and, as the
tracks are now arranged, the o. r.
& N. trains could come direct to
the Great Northern depot without
any additional trackage or expense.


This matter Is one that is inter-
esting the various hotels and cab
lines, as a move of this kind would
tend to centralize business in way
that would be great saving to the
city transportation companies.


Nothing in the way of a denial or
confirmation could be had from the
local offices Of the lines mentioned.


There would be no necessity, it is
urged. for any expense in making
this change. as all needed switches
are already in place at the present
junction Of the two roads.
